writeup: The Buffalo Bills held Day 2 of their mandatory minicamp on June 10, 2026, with several key storylines emerging from the practice field. Safety Cole Bishop remained sidelined following an offseason knee procedure, raising questions about his availability for training camp, though the team expressed optimism about his recovery timeline. New addition C.J. Gardner-Johnson immediately made his presence felt, described as 'loud' and tone-setting for a Bills defense looking to establish a new identity. Wide receiver Keon Coleman turned heads with his performance, showcasing more than just his trademark physicality and signaling a potential breakout role in the offense. James Cook also hit a notable milestone during the session, adding another positive note to an otherwise injury-shadowed day.
